author: michaelmassoni
description: A collection of tools for cyber security analysts.

manifest.author: Michael Massoni
manifest.description: A collection of tools for SOC analysts.
manifest.name: SOC Toolkit

name: SOC Toolkit

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## Release Notes - v1.6.2

### Bug Fixes
- Context Menu IP Check: Fixed an issue where using "Check IP reputation" from the right-click menu on a selection with multiple IPs would only process the first IP. It now correctly processes all IPs in the highlighted selection.

### Improvements
- Result Indentation: Improved the formatting of reputation results.
  - If the IP address is in a bulleted list, the results are now indented with a tab to properly nest under the IP.
  - If the IP address is on a plain line, the results are bulleted at the same level.
releases.name: v1.6.2 - Bug Fixes

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## ✨ New & Improved
### Now available in the Obsidian Community Plugins browser!
- Install directly from Obsidian: Settings → Community plugins → Browse → Search for “SOC Toolkit”

## 📝 Documentation
### Updated the Installation section:
- Instructions now reflect the new, simpler Community Plugins installation process.
### Updated the Configuration section:
- Clarified API key setup and added instructions for testing keys.
- Minor improvements to usage and roadmap documentation.
## 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fixed: Only one IP in a highlighted selection would get annotated.
- Fixed: Non-defanged (fanged?) IPv4 addresses were not being annotated.

## Upgrade recommended for all users!
- If you installed manually before, you can now safely switch to the Community Plugins version for easier one-click updates within Obsidian.
releases.name: v1.6.1 - Community Plugin

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## ✨ New & Improved
### IPv6 Defanging Improved:
- Only single colons (:) in IPv6 addresses are now defanged to [:].
- Double colons (::) are left untouched to avoid Markdown rendering issues and maintain readability.

### Settings UI Polish:
- Section headings in the settings tab now use native Obsidian heading styles for a more consistent look.
- "Example output", "VirusTotal", and "AbuseIPDB" headings have been streamlined or removed for clarity.

### Dependency & Build Improvements:
- Added obsidian and tslib as dev dependencies for better type support and build reliability.
- Cleaned up unnecessary debug logging for a tidier codebase.

## 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fixed an issue where defanged IPv6 addresses like :: would render as broken Markdown links.

Upgrade recommended for all users, especially those working with IPv6 addresses or customising plugin output.
releases.name: v1.6.0 - Settings UI Improvements

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## New Features
- Added support for defanged IP addresses in IP reputation checks
  - Fully defanged IPs (e.g. `8[.]8[.]8[.]8`)
  - IPs with only the last dot defanged (e.g. `8.8.8[.]8`)
- Added new "Defang IPs in Current Note" command
- Added right-click menu option for defanging IP addresses

## Improvements
- Enhanced IP detection to handle various defanging formats
- Better error handling and logging for IP processing

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed issue with IP reputation checks not working for defanged IPs
- Improved content replacement logic for annotations
- Fixed annotation detection to prevent duplicate entries

## Technical Details
- Updated IP detection regex patterns to support defanged formats
- Enhanced content processing to maintain note formatting
- Added comprehensive logging for better debugging

## Usage
The plugin now supports checking reputation for both regular and defanged IP addresses. You can:
1. Use the "Check IP Reputation" command on defanged IPs
2. Use the new "Defang IPs in Current Note" command to defang IPs
3. Right-click on IPs to access defanging options
releases.name: v1.5.0 - Defanged IP Support

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
This version is a minor update containing a few bug fixes.

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed an issue with AbuseIPDB country names returning as blank
  - The {countryName} output option now shows full country names instead of country codes, by checking the country code returned by the API against a list in the main.ts file
  - Country codes can be included in the output by adding the {countryCode} field if desired
- Added "N/A" output rather than blank for VirusTotal {tags} when none are present
- Fixed an issue with AbuseIPDB {lastReported} showing "just now" when there are no reports
  - This will no longer be included in the output if no reports are present
- Fixed a caching issue where output format changes weren't being applied to cached results
  - Now if you update your output settings and re-run the plugin on the same IP, the correct results will be displayed
releases.name: v1.4.1 - Bug Fixes

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## Core Changes
- In this update, I have renamed the plugin to "SOC Toolkit", to more accurately represent that this plugin will be more than just IP reputation analysis. I plan to keep developing this plugin to make it an all-in-one tool for SOC analysts and cyber security professionals.

## New Features:
- Added "Check IP Reputation in Highlighted Area" command
- Added right-click context menu for quick IP checks
- Improved handling of multiple IPs in highlighted text
- Added support for IPs in markdown lists (e.g., "- 192.168.1.1")

## Improvements:
- Better error handling for API responses
- Improved sanitisation of selected IP addresses
- Enhanced documentation with hotkey recommendations

## Documentation:
- Updated README with new plugin name and description
- Added detailed usage instructions
- Added roadmap for future features
- Improved installation guide

## Planned Features:
- IP/URL defanging
- Domain/URL reputation analysis
- File hash analysis
- And more!
releases.name: v1.4.0 - SOC Toolkit

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
This release focuses on improving the settings interface and user experience:

## Core Changes
- CSS was moved to a dedicated styles.css file. This will allow for easier management of styling, and was required as apart of approval in the Obsidian Community Plugins list.

## UI Improvements:
- Reorganised settings page with clear section headings
- Added visual separation between VirusTotal and AbuseIPDB sections
- Improved API key input field sizing
- Enhanced field tag tooltips with indicators

## Settings Enhancements:
- Simplified toggle labels for better clarity
- Added real-time example output preview, no need to manually refresh
- Improved field tag descriptions and hover behavior
- Better visual hierarchy in settings layout

## Bug Fixes:
- Fixed tooltip positioning to prevent overflow
- Improved settings page responsiveness
releases.name: v1.3.0 - Styling Updates & Live Preview

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## New Features:
- Revamped settings page with dedicated API Settings section
- Added preview example output with manual refresh button

## UI Improvements:
- Streamlined settings layout for better organisation
- Enhanced readability with proper spacing and typography
releases.name: v1.2.0 - Enhanced Settings UI and Output Customisation

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
## New Features
- Added support for IPv6 address detection and analysis
- Improved IP address detection with more accurate regex patterns
- Enhanced handling of compressed IPv6 addresses (e.g., `2001:4860:4860::8888`)

## Technical Improvements
- Refactored IP detection logic for better reliability
- Added comprehensive debug logging for troubleshooting
- Improved handling of edge cases in IP address formats

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed issue with partial IPv6 address detection
- Resolved problem with compressed IPv6 address handling
- Improved accuracy of public IP address filtering
releases.name: v1.1.0 - IPv6 Support

releases.author: michaelmassoni
releases.description:
Initial release of the IP Reputation Checker plugin for Obsidian.

Features:
- Scan notes for public IPv4 addresses
- Check IP reputation using VirusTotal API
- Check IP reputation using AbuseIPDB API
- Display reputation results directly in your notes
- Automatic updates of reputation data
- Configurable API keys in settings

Requirements:
- Obsidian v0.15.0 or higher
- VirusTotal API key (free tier available)
- AbuseIPDB API key (free tier available)

For more information, visit the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/michaelmassoni/obsidian-ip-analysis-plugin).
releases.name: v1.0.0 - Initial Release
